The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

parameters.description: Filter invoices issued on or after the specified date.

parameters.name: issued_to_date
parameters.description: Filter invoices issued before the specified date.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: If status query has value of all, all invoices will be returned. Otherwise only paid invoices will be fetched.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records to retrieve per page.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Index of the first record to return.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Return next page of results after the given cursor.

parameters.description: Will include invoices from COR workers in the response.

get.description:
Returns a cursor-paginated list of payments made to Deel, including worker details, payment status, and payment methods. Use the `next_cursor` value from the response to retrieve subsequent pages.
 **Token scopes**: `accounting:read`

parameters.name: date_from
parameters.description: Filters results to include payments created on or after this date (in ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: date_to
parameters.description: Filters results to include payments created before this date (in ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A cursor for pagination. Use the value returned in the 'next_cursor' field to get the next page of results.

parameters.name: currencies
parameters.description: Filters payments by their currency codes. Can be a single currency code or an array of codes.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: Filters payments by legal entity type (e.g., 'individual' or 'company'). Can be a single entity type or an array.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Filters payments by their status (e.g., 'paid' or 'processing'). Can be a single status type or an array.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:

The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

post.summary: Associate application with an interview plan stage
post.description:
Use this endpoint to associate an application with a specific interview plan stage in the hiring workflow. Use it when moving a candidate into a stage or backfilling history. Set is_current_stage to true to update the application's current stage; set it to false to record a historical entry without changing the current stage. To control triggered activities, pass applicable_job_activities; omit it to trigger all activities or pass an empty array to trigger none. If the candidate is being archived, include candidate_archivation_reason_id and optionally candidate_archivation_email_template_id to send a rejection email.
 **Token scopes**: `ats:write`

parameters.name: application_id
parameters.description: ID of the application to be associated with the interview plan stage

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl

  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

201.description: Application interview plan stage successfully created

400.description: Email body contains placeholders that can't be resolved

 **Token scopes**: `ats:read`

parameters.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the nextCursor value from the previous response to get the next page of results.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of applications to return per page

parameters.name: search_text
parameters.description: Search text to filter applications by candidate name or email

parameters.name: job_id
parameters.description: Filter applications by specific job ID

parameters.name: interview_plan_stage_ids
parameters.description: Filter applications by specific interview plan stage IDs

parameters.name: candidate_tag_ids
parameters.description: Filter applications by candidate tag IDs

parameters.name: source_slugs
parameters.description: Filter applications by source slugs

parameters.name: job_criterias_matchings
parameters.description: Filter applications by job criteria matching state. Each object specifies a job criteria ID and whether the candidate matches it.

parameters.name: current_stage_category_type_slugs
parameters.description: Filter applications by current stage category type slugs

parameters.name: current_stage_default_type_slugs
parameters.description: Filter applications by current stage default type slugs. Use 'others' for custom stages.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Filter applications updated after this timestamp (ISO 8601 format)

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Field to sort applications by

parameters.name: sort_order

400.description: Request validation failed. A required field is missing, has an invalid format, or the worker is not eligible for equity exercise.

401.description: Authentication failed. The access token is missing, expired, or invalid.

403.description: Access denied. The authenticated user does not have the required contracts:write scope.

404.description: The worker profile associated with the given worker_id was not found.

422.description: A request with the same exercise_id already exists. Each exercise_id can only be used once.

500.description: Internal server error. Possible causes include missing EOR contract for the worker, FX rate service failure, or database errors.

fmv.description: The fair market value of the equity.

quantity.description: The quantity of equity to exercise. Must be a positive number.

issuer_id.description: The unique identifier of the issuer.

worker_id.description: The public UUID of the worker. Must correspond to an existing worker in the system.

address_zip.description: The zip code of the address.

exercise_id.description: The unique identifier of the exercise.

stakeholder_id.description: The unique identifier of the stakeholder.

option_grant_id.description: The numeric identifier of the option grant. Must contain only digits.

fmv_currency_code.description: The currency code of the fair market value (ISO 4217).

stakeholder_email.description: The email address of the stakeholder.

option_grant_issue.description: The issue of the option grant.

option_grant_label.description: The label of the option grant.

option_grant_currency.description: The currency of the option grant (ISO 4217).

option_grant_issue_date.description: The issue date of the option grant (ISO 8601 date).

option_grant_expiry_date.description: The expiry date of the option grant (ISO 8601 date).

option_grant_strike_price.description: The strike price of the option grant.

post.description:
Generate a one-time magic link that allows a manager to log in to Deel without entering credentials. The target manager must belong to the requesting organization and cannot be an organization admin. Only available for White Label enabled organizations or Partners or Child organizations. The magic link expires after 5 minutes and can only be used once. After clicking the link, the manager will be automatically authenticated and redirected to Deel.
 **Token scopes**: `auth:write`

get.description:
Retrieves custom field values for a specific organization structure (team). This endpoint returns all custom fields configured for organization structures, including their current values, inheritance status, and any pending change requests. Custom fields can be inherited from parent teams in the organizational hierarchy, and the response indicates whether each value is inherited or set directly on the specified team. If the user has permission to view data change requests, pending changes will also be included in the response.

post.description:
Creates a new organization along with:

- An API token scoped to the new organization. This token can be used to perform API operations on behalf of the new organization.
- A default group for the organization.
- A hierarchy linking the authenticated organization (the Parent Organization) with the new one (the Child Organization).
- A user from the parent organization who will be assigned to the child organization.

This endpoint sets up the bare minimum required for the new organization to be operational immediately after creation.
 **Token scopes**: `organizations:write`

parameters.description: Return a page of results with given number of records.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Return a page of results with given number of records.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Include a contract if by name or contractor name contains given search term.

parameters.name: teams
parameters.description: Filter the results based on the team(group) the users contract is associated with

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Pick the fields you want to see in the response in order to avoid a bloated people list output. Supports dot notation (e.g., 'employments[0].contract_status') to access nested fields.

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort people by given field name.

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Order direction of results; ascending or descending.

parameters.name: hiring_statuses
parameters.description: Employee's current hiring status.

parameters.name: band_placements
parameters.description: Employee's current band placement.

parameters.name: comparative_ratios_from
parameters.description: Employee's minimum band placement

parameters.name: comparative_ratios_to
parameters.description: Employee's maximum band placement

parameters.name: hiring_types
parameters.description: Filter people by hiring types

parameters.name: person_statuses
parameters.description: Filter people by person (worker profile) lifecycle status.

get.description:
This endpoint allows organizations managing workers on Deel to retrieve detailed KYC verification data using the worker’s profile ID. It provides comprehensive information including verification status, document type, submission, approval, rejection, and expiry dates, supporting compliance monitoring and onboarding workflows
 **Token scopes**: `screenings:read`

parameters.name: worker_profile_id
parameters.description: Worker's HRIS profile public ID client requests to fetch KYC details for. This parameter is mutually exclusive with profile_id

parameters.name: contract_id
parameters.description: Worker's contract ID. This parameter is required in case there are multiple profiles associated to the target worker. For example, when worker used to work for the former company he had another profile ID. So we need contract ID to properly resolve profile's KYC details.  In case this parameter is not provided and multiple associated profiles are found, exception with 409 status code will be thrown

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.description: Worker's profile public ID client requests to fetch KYC details for, This parameter is mutually exclusive with worker_profile_id

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

400.description: Bad request - Invalid worker profile ID

403.description: Forbidden - User not authorized to access this resource

404.description: Not Found - We could not extract any profile by the provided hris profile

409.description: Conflict - We found multiple profiles for the provided worker ID. Please provide the contract id to resolve KYC details

post.description:
Synchronize time off requests from an external HRIS to Deel. Only Global Payroll contracts are allowed for this operation. This endpoint can perform 2 different operations with the provided time off list: i) upsert - time off requests identified by an external ID will be either inserted or updated at Deel. ii) delete - existing time off requests identified by an external ID with the parameter 'deleted: true' will be deleted. Deel will identify the impacts of each operation on each payroll cycle.
 **Token scopes**: `time-off:write`

get.description:
Use this endpoint to access a worker's transaction history quickly. This integration works with white-label clients and ensures compliance by not following the generic contractor route
 **Token scopes**: `worker:read`

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of transactions to return in this page (page size).

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Subset of transactions by processing state. Public enum is UPPER_SNAKE; the server lowercases for validation (legacy lowercase query values are still accepted).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Lower bound on transaction `created_at` (inclusive), ISO-8601 date-time.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Upper bound on transaction `created_at` (inclusive), ISO-8601 date-time.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from the previous response field `next_cursor`. Omit on the first page; pass the last `next_cursor` value as `cursor` to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Filter listed transactions to these mapped status values (applied after fetch; may affect how rows align with `total_count`).

parameters.name: amount_from
parameters.description: Minimum transaction amount to include.

parameters.name: amount_to
parameters.description: Maximum transaction amount to include.

parameters.name: currencies
parameters.description: Only include transactions in one of these currency codes.

parameters.name: transaction_type
parameters.description: Restrict to pay-in or pay-out. Public enum is UPPER_SNAKE; the server lowercases for validation (legacy lowercase query values are still accepted).

parameters.name: withdrawal_methods
parameters.description: Filter by withdrawal / payout method identifiers.

parameters.name: last_days
parameters.description: Only include transactions created in the last N days.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Additional transaction type filter (backend-specific string).

parameters.name: include_running_total
parameters.description: When true, each row may include a running total over the result set.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

field_type.description: Use this value to select the appropriate UI input component. Represents the raw input type from the field configuration. Known values include 'text', 'date', 'select', 'textarea', 'tel', 'phone', 'number', 'checkbox', and 'string'. Additional values may exist.

description.description: Use this value to display supplementary context about the field below or beside the input. HTML tags are stripped. Returns an empty string when no description is configured.

helper_text.description: Use this value to display footnote or instructional text near the field. HTML tags are stripped. Returns an empty string when no helper text is configured.

type.description: Use this value to determine which validation strategy to apply. "REGEX" indicates the 'value' field contains a regular expression pattern. Any other value indicates a custom validation type identified by its uppercase name.

value.description: Use this value as the validation parameter. For REGEX type, contains the regular expression pattern to validate the field input against. Absent when the validation type is a custom type that does not require a parameter.

error_message.description: Use this message to display to the user when the field value fails validation. Defaults to 'Review the value entered' when no custom message is configured.

FormsGpWorkerAdditionalFieldsCountryCode.description: Use this operator to evaluate the dependency condition against the referenced field's submitted value.

op.description: Use this operator to evaluate the dependency condition against the referenced field's submitted value.

value.description: Use this value as the operand for the dependency condition evaluation.

FormsGpWorkerAdditionalFieldsCountryCode.description: Use this value to evaluate the dependency condition. When the value is a primitive (string, number, or boolean), the condition is satisfied when the referenced field equals this value. When the value is an object with 'op' and 'value' properties, apply the specified operator: 'eq' (equals), 'ne' (not equals), 'gt' (greater than), 'lt' (less than), 'notnull' (field is not null), 'contains' (field value contains the specified substring).
FormsGpWorkerAdditionalFieldsCountryCode.title: FormsGpWorkerAdditionalFieldsCountryCodeGetResponsesContentApplicationJsonSchemaDataItemsDependenciesItemsValue

key.description: Use this value to identify the field that this dependency refers to. Contains the snake_case key of the dependency field, matching another field's 'key' property in the same response.

value.description: Use this value to evaluate the dependency condition. When the value is a primitive (string, number, or boolean), the condition is satisfied when the referenced field equals this value. When the value is an object with 'op' and 'value' properties, apply the specified operator: 'eq' (equals), 'ne' (not equals), 'gt' (greater than), 'lt' (less than), 'notnull' (field is not null), 'contains' (field value contains the specified substring).

key.description: Use this value as the field identifier when submitting additional information. Represents the snake_case version of the internal field name, generated by the API naming convention service.

type.description: Use this value to determine the data type expected when submitting field values. Mapped from the raw input type: 'date', 'text', 'select', 'textarea', 'tel', 'phone', and 'string' map to "string"; 'number' maps to "number"; 'checkbox' maps to "boolean". An empty string indicates an unrecognized input type.

ui_guide.description: Use this object to control how the field is rendered in the UI. Contains labels, input type, display order, and supplementary text.

validation.description: Use this array to apply client-side validation before form submission. Contains zero or one validation rule per field. An empty array means no validation is configured.

is_required.description: Use this flag to determine whether the field must be provided when submitting additional information. For GP workers, the value considers the GP-specific optionality setting when available, falling back to the general optionality flag.

required_by.description: Use this value to determine at which stage in the payroll lifecycle this field must be collected. Known values: 'before_payroll' (required before payroll processing), 'after_payroll' (can be collected after initial payroll), 'contract_signing' (required at contract signing). Absent from the response when no lifecycle stage is configured.

dependencies.description: Use this array to implement conditional field visibility. When non-empty, this field should only be displayed and required when the referenced dependency conditions are met. An empty array means the field has no conditional dependencies.

values_allowed.description: Use this array to populate dropdown or select options when field_type is 'select'. Only present when the field has predefined options. Absent from the response for free-text, date, number, and other non-select field types.

data.description: Use this array to build the form UI. Each item defines one field with its type, validation, and display configuration. Items are pre-sorted by EDM display order. An empty array means no additional fields are configured for the requested country.

message.description: Use this message to detect rate limiting. Implement exponential backoff before retrying.

errors.description: Use this array to identify each validation failure. Each item describes one parameter that failed validation.

message.description: Use this message to understand why the request failed. Indicates the country does not have an additional info template configured.
Use this message for logging and debugging purposes. Contains a general error description.

reason.description: Reason for taking time off. This is complementary information for the time-off description. Useful to provide sub categories for the same policy.

status.description: Status of the time off request. Can be REQUESTED, APPROVED, REJECTED, USED or CANCELED.

is_paid.description: Whether the time off is paid or unpaid.

end_date.description: End date of the time off request. This is the date when the time off ends. For example, if you take 2 days off, this will be the end date of the second day.

created_at.description: This is the date when the time off request was created in the database.

start_date.description: Start date of the time off request. This is the date when the time off starts. For example, if you take 2 days off, this will be the start date of the first day.

approved_at.description: This is the date when the time off request was approved. This will be null if the time off request is not approved yet.

half_end_date.description: Whether the end date is a half day or not.

time_off_type.description: The type of time off taken.

deduction_amount.description: Deduction amount. Available for independent contractor's policies

entitlement_unit.description: The unit in which the time off usage and entitlement are calculated.

time_off_dailies.description: Used to break down the time off request into daily records. Each entry represents a single day within the time off period, allowing for detailed tracking of working days, non-working days, and holidays included in the request.

time_off_type_id.description: Time off type id. This is the id of the time off type associated with the time off request.

recipient_profile.description: The time off profile of the recipient of the time off request. Must be a worker profile.

requester_profile.description: The time off profile of the requester of the time off request. Can be either a client or worker profile.

time_off_percentage.description: Used to specify the percentage of time off taken relative to full-time employment. This value ranges from 0 to 1, where 1 represents full-time (100%) and 0.5 represents part-time (50%). This is useful for part-time workers or when taking partial time off.

is_end_date_estimated.description: Used to indicate if the end date is an estimate rather than a confirmed date. This is particularly important for event-based policies such as Parental Leave, where the actual end date may depend on the actual birth or adoption date, which may not be known at the time of request submission.

other_type_description.description: Used to provide a custom description when the time off type is categorized as 'OTHER'. This allows users to specify the nature of time off that doesn't fit into standard categories, providing flexibility for unique or organization-specific time off types.

deelToken.description:
## Authentication
The Deel API uses bearer tokens to authenticate requests. All API calls must be made over HTTPS — calls over plain HTTP or without authentication will fail.

```curl
curl -X GET 'https://api.letsdeel.com/rest/v2/contracts' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR-TOKEN-HERE'
```

[Learn more about authentication](/api/authentication)

oauth2.description: Standard OAuth2 security scheme based on https://swagger.io/docs/specification/authentication/
